dialogueChunks.intro: It's worse than we thought, {player_name}. The suspicion has turned violent.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Someone vandalized {target_npc}'s workshop: smashed tools, scattered supplies, painted threats across the door. {target_npc} is too shaken to leave. Meanwhile, the real culprit behind the {quest_focus} is still out there. The {enemy_type} have been spotted {location_hint} where the stolen {quest_item} was likely taken.
dialogueChunks.outro: The {quest_stakes} {quest_stakes_is} crumbling by the hour. If we don't produce the truth soon, {target_npc} won't have a home to return to.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the accusers have found new evidence. Or rather, they've manufactured it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Someone planted stolen {quest_item} in {target_npc}'s home and made sure the council found it. The framing is crude but effective. I know it was planted because the items were taken from a cache {location_hint} that was raided by {enemy_type} days before. If you can find the real raider camp and recover what remains, we can prove the timeline doesn't add up.
dialogueChunks.outro: Someone wants {target_npc} gone badly enough to frame them. We need to find out who before the council votes. The {quest_threat} {quest_threat_is} feeding on itself.

dialogueChunks.intro: {target_npc} has been confined by the council, {player_name}. Time is running out.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The council gave us three days to produce counter-evidence or they'll proceed with banishment. The real trail leads {location_hint}, where {quest_item} was last seen before the {enemy_type} carried it off. If I can put the actual stolen goods next to the planted ones, even the most stubborn accuser will see the difference.
dialogueChunks.outro: Three days, {player_name}. That's all the {quest_stakes} {quest_stakes_has} left.
